name: Castillo de Asmesnal and Alfaraz de Sayago Loop
name_original: Castillo del Asmesnal desde Santiz — circular por Alfaraz de Sayago y Moraleja de Sayago
description: Explore the Sayago region on this 32.6-mile moderate mountain bike loop from Santiz, featuring the Castillo del Asmesnal Ruins.
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Santiz traverse a landscape characterized by rolling hills and historical routes. The region features paths like the Vía Verde de la Plata, suggesting former railway lines converted into greenways, often providing relatively flat and accessible terrain. Riders can expect to encounter areas with pine forests and open countryside, with some routes leading past historical landmarks such as castles. The elevation changes are generally moderate, offering a mix of climbs and descents suitable for various skill levels. Best mountain bike trails around Santiz The most popular mountain bike trail is Peñausende — circular por la Vía Verde de la Plata, a 29.5 miles (47.4 km) trail that takes 3 hours 48 minutes to complete. This route follows a greenway, offering a smooth ride through the landscape. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Castillo de Asmesnal and Alfaraz de Sayago Loop, a moderate 32.6 miles (52.5 km) path. This route explores the countryside, passing by historical castle ruins and through varied terrain. Local mountain bikers also love the Pinar loop from Valdelosa, a 24.9 miles (40.0 km) trail leading through pine forests, often completed in about 3 hours 20 minutes. Mountain biking around Santiz is defined by greenways, pine forests, and routes connecting historical sites. The network offers options for different ability levels, including moderate and difficult routes. The routes in Santiz are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 2 reviews. More than 30 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Santiz's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Santiz?
A: There are 7 mountain bike trails around Santiz listed on komoot, offering a variety of experiences through rolling hills and historical landscapes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around Santiz?
A: The trails in Santiz traverse a landscape of rolling hills, often following historical routes like the Vía Verde de la Plata. You'll encounter pine forests and open countryside, with moderate elevation changes suitable for various skill levels.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails in Santiz for beginners?
A: While there are no trails specifically graded as 'easy' in Santiz, many routes are described as 'moderate' and include sections like greenways which offer a smoother ride. For example, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1282104855/penausende-circular-por-la-via-verde-de-la-plata" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñausende — circular por la Vía Verde de la Plata&lt;/a&gt; follows a greenway, providing relatively accessible terrain.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders in Santiz?
A: Yes, for experienced riders seeking a challenge, there is one trail graded as 'difficult'. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19807908" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Peñausende – Wind Turbines on Teso Santo loop from Peñausende&lt;/a&gt; is a longer route with more significant elevation changes, offering a demanding ride.

Q: What are some popular circular mountain bike routes in Santiz?
A: Several popular routes in Santiz are circular. These include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1282104855/penausende-circular-por-la-via-verde-de-la-plata" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñausende — circular por la Vía Verde de la Plata&lt;/a&gt;, a 29.5-mile route, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1282165115/castillo-de-asmesnal-and-alfaraz-de-sayago-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Asmesnal and Alfaraz de Sayago Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 32.6 miles.

Q: What historical landmarks or points of interest can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: The Santiz region is rich in history. Along the trails, you can encounter historical landmarks such as the ruins of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5594631" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Asmesnal&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6063542" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Peñausende&lt;/a&gt;. Some routes also pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6063809" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Wind Turbines on Teso Santo&lt;/a&gt;, offering unique views.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Santiz?
A: The mountain bike routes in Santiz are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars. Over 30 mountain bikers have explored the varied terrain, often praising the greenways, pine forests, and routes connecting historical sites.

Q: Are there any mountain bike routes that follow greenways in Santiz?
A: Yes, the Vía Verde de la Plata is a prominent feature in the region. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1282104855/penausende-circular-por-la-via-verde-de-la-plata" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñausende — circular por la Vía Verde de la Plata&lt;/a&gt; is a popular route that follows this greenway, offering a smooth ride through the landscape.

Q: How long are the mountain bike trails in Santiz?
A: The mountain bike trails in Santiz vary in length. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1282104855/penausende-circular-por-la-via-verde-de-la-plata" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñausende — circular por la Vía Verde de la Plata&lt;/a&gt; is 29.5 miles (47.4 km), while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1282165115/castillo-de-asmesnal-and-alfaraz-de-sayago-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Asmesnal and Alfaraz de Sayago Loop&lt;/a&gt; is 32.6 miles (52.5 km).

Q: Can I find routes that go through pine forests?
A: Absolutely. The region features areas with beautiful pine forests. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31864331" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pinar loop from Valdelosa&lt;/a&gt; is a 24.9-mile (40.0 km) trail specifically designed to lead you through these scenic pine forests.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by churches or religious buildings?
A: Yes, some routes may pass by local religious sites. For instance, you can find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3513669" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zamayón Church&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6063772" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church and Town Hall of Moraleja de Sayago&lt;/a&gt; as points of interest in the vicinity of the trails.
tips: On the outskirts of the town of Santiz, you come across the ruins of the castle of Asmesnal. Medieval defensive castle that played a fundamental role in the area's war with Portugal in the 15th century.
Moraleja de Sayago is a small rural town in the Sayago region located next to the Moraleja river. The church and the town hall in the center of the city.
Beautiful gravel path that skirts the ridge until reaching the highest point of the Teso Santo, where the communication antennas are located. The trail continues following the wind farms.

An authentic living cathedral worth visiting. Near the town of Santiz, we find this hundred-year-old cork oak specimen, which is estimated to be around 600 years old. It is part of the Route of the Living Cathedrals
It is accessed through a metal door that is closed to protect the tree.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Castillo del Asmesnal mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as a moderate mountain bike ride. It requires good fitness and advanced riding skills. Some sections may be challenging, potentially requiring you to dismount and push your bike. On Komoot's scale, this typically corresponds to an STS S2 difficulty level.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied, typical of rural Spanish mountain biking. You can expect a mix of dirt roads, tracks, and possibly some singletrack. While specific details are not extensively provided, similar regions often feature rocky paths and potentially loose or sandy sections, interspersed with climbs and descents.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The primary highlight is the Castillo de Asmesnal Ruins, a significant medieval fortification offering historical interest. As you pass through villages like Alfaraz de Sayago and Moraleja de Sayago, you'll also see the Church and Town Hall of Moraleja de Sayago. Other notable features include the Wind Turbines on Teso Santo and the Alcornoque Gordo de Calahorra.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this mountain bike route in Santiz?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas in Santiz are not detailed, as a circular route starting and ending in the village, you should look for public parking options within Santiz. It's advisable to arrive early, especially on weekends, to secure a spot.

faqs.question: Is the Castillo del Asmesnal route suitable for mountain biking with dogs?
faqs.answer: Generally, mountain biking with dogs is permitted on public trails in this region. However, it's crucial to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through villages, near livestock, or in areas with wildlife. Always carry water for your dog and be mindful of their endurance on a 52.5 km ride.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay entrance fees for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits or have to pay entrance fees to ride this mountain bike route. It uses public roads and trails through the Spanish countryside.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to ride this trail is typically during the spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) months. During these periods, the weather is generally milder and more pleasant for mountain biking. Summers can be very hot in this region, and winters can be cold and wet.

faqs.question: Are there any services like restaurants or shops along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the route passes through villages such as Alfaraz de Sayago and Moraleja de Sayago, you can expect to find some local services. These may include small shops, bars, or restaurants where you can refuel and rest. It's always a good idea to carry enough water and snacks, especially on a longer ride like this.

faqs.question: Does this mountain bike route intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several local paths. You will ride along parts of the Camino Asmesnal, Camino Lomo Judío, Camino de los Contrabandistas, Rodera del Lomo, Camino de las Burras, Camino de Ronda, Camino de Ledesma, Camino de Alfaraz, Vereda de Ledesma a Zamora, Calle Trasera Iglesia, and Camino de Peñausende.

faqs.question: Is this a good route for beginner mountain bikers or families with children?
faqs.answer: Given its classification as a moderate mountain bike ride requiring good fitness and advanced skills, this route is generally not recommended for beginners or young children. Some sections may be challenging and require technical ability or even dismounting. Families or beginners might prefer shorter, less technical routes in the area.

faqs.question: How long does it take to complete the Castillo del Asmesnal desde Santiz mountain bike loop?
faqs.answer: The estimated duration for completing this 52.5 km mountain bike loop is approximately 4 hours and 6 minutes. This duration can vary depending on your fitness level, the number of stops you make, and the specific trail conditions on the day of your ride.
